user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to replace your current laptop because it has reached end of life, and during the same call you also want to request a standing desk converter for your workstation.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a couple of IT requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your replacement laptop request is successfully submitted, you receive the request ID, and you are told the return instructions and return deadline for your current laptop.
Your standing desk converter request is successfully submitted and you receive the request ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number, and give a brief high-level summary that you need help with a laptop replacement and a standing desk converter. Do not volunteer detailed preferences until the agent asks.

2. For the first intent, if the agent starts with the laptop replacement, say your current laptop is end-of-life when asked for the reason. If the agent asks what you want, provide your preferred operating system as Windows and your preferred laptop size as 16-inch. If asked for delivery details, provide Headquarters and FL2. If the agent reads back the request details before submitting, confirm them exactly if they match.

3. If the agent instead starts with the standing desk converter first, state that you want a standing desk converter when asked what equipment you need. If asked for delivery details, provide Headquarters and FL2. If the agent asks about an ergonomic assessment, state that you believe you already have one on file and wait for them to verify it. If the agent reads back the equipment request details before submitting, confirm them exactly if they match.

4. After the first intent is completed or explicitly blocked, raise the second intent if the agent has not already handled it. For the standing desk converter request, use standing_desk_converter as the equipment type and Headquarters FL2 as the delivery location.
5. Once both requests have been completed and you have concrete confirmation for each, thank the agent briefly and end the call.

When the agent provides confirmation numbers or request IDs, listen for both requests separately. For the laptop replacement, also listen for the asset return deadline and instructions. If the agent gives a read-back of either ID or detail, acknowledge only if it matches what was submitted.

If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with information defined in information_required. If the question is not necessary for either request and the answer is not defined here, say you do not have any other details to add and ask to continue with the request.
Do not volunteer extra context, do not invent urgency, and do not add any third request. Wait for the agent to finish one intent before moving to the next if they are handling them sequentially.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a request ID for your laptop replacement, you have been told the return instructions and the return deadline for your current laptop, and you have also received a request ID for your standing desk converter request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If asked which operating system you want on the replacement laptop, choose Windows.
If asked what laptop size you want, choose 16-inch.
If asked what ergonomic equipment you want, choose standing_desk_converter.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have two facilities requests to handle in one call: you want parking in the North Lot at Headquarters, and if no space is available you want to be added to that parking waitlist; then you need a conference room for 10 people at Headquarters on 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30, preferably on floor 2, but if floor 2 cannot accommodate the meeting you will accept a room on floor 3 that fits everyone.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a couple of workplace requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: You are successfully added to the North Lot parking waitlist and receive a waitlist ID or equivalent confirmation reference.

You have a confirmed conference room booking for 10 people at Headquarters on 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30 in a room on floor 3, and you receive a booking request ID or confirmation plus a calendar invite confirmation.
nice_to_have_criteria: You would like an actual parking space in the North Lot, but if you are told no spaces are available there, ask one follow-up question about whether there is any current availability in that lot; if the answer is still no, accept being waitlisted.

You would like a conference room on floor 2 for 10 people on 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30, but if you are told floor 2 rooms are too small or already booked, ask one follow-up question confirming there is nothing suitable on floor 2; if the answer is still no, accept a room on floor 3 that fits 10.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number when asked. Do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.
2. When the Agent asks what requests you have, briefly say you have two facilities requests: parking first, then a conference room. Do not give all specifics at once unless the agent asks.

3. First intent — parking: when asked, say you want parking in the North Lot at Headquarters. If the agent asks for the zone, provide the zone code for the North Lot: PZB. Wait for the agent to check availability before saying anything else.

4. If the agent says there are no parking spaces available in North Lot, ask exactly one follow-up question to confirm whether there is any availability at all in North Lot right now. If the answer is still no, accept being placed on the waitlist for North Lot immediately.

5. If the agent offers parking in other zones before offering the waitlist, decline alternate zones and restate that if no spaces are available in the North Lot, you want to be added to the North Lot waitlist. Do not evaluate parking options in any other zones besides the North Lot.
6. Confirm the waitlist placement only after the agent reads back that it is for parking in North Lot / PZB and gives you a waitlist ID or queue confirmation.

7. Second intent — conference room: after the parking request is completed or explicitly deferred, say you need a conference room at Headquarters for 10 people on 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30, preferably on floor FL2. Do not mention any floor-3 fallback until the agent tells you FL2 cannot satisfy the request.

If the agent asks whether you have any required room equipment, say you have no required equipment.

8. If the agent says no FL2 room works because the rooms are too small or already booked, ask exactly one follow-up question confirming there is nothing on FL2 that can hold 10 people for that date and time. If the answer is still no, ask the agent to look for a room on FL3. If the agent offers to check any or all floors in the Headquarters building, say that you specifically want FL3.

9. If multiple FL3 rooms are offered that all fit 10 people for 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30, choose deterministically: select the room with the smallest capacity that is still at least 10. If there is a tie on capacity, choose the room with the lexicographically earliest room code. If only one qualifying FL3 room is offered, accept it.

10. If the agent offers a room on a different date or time, decline and restate the required date 2026-11-16 and time 13:00 to 14:30. Only accept alternatives that keep the same date and exact time window; the only allowed fallback is changing the floor from FL2 to FL3. No fallback or change is allowed for the date or time.

11. Before final confirmation of the room, listen to the read-back and confirm it only if the booking is for Headquarters, on 2026-11-16, from 13:00 to 14:30, for 10 attendees, and the selected room is on FL3.

12. Wrap up the call only after both intents have reached their final state: you are successfully added to the waitlist for North Lot parking, and the conference room is successfully booked on FL3 with confirmation evidence.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been told that no parking space is available in North Lot, you have successfully been added to the North Lot/PZB parking waitlist with a waitlist ID or confirmation reference, you have been told that no suitable room is available on FL2 for 10 people at the requested time, and you have a confirmed booking for a room on FL3 for 2026-11-16 from 13:00 to 14:30 for 10 attendees with a booking request ID or confirmation and calendar invite confirmation. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either request for 3 consecutive turns, says the waitlist cannot be created and no parking alternative process exists, or says the room booking cannot be processed and no qualifying FL3 room can be booked, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: The scenario allows escalation to a live agent. However, if the agent can place you on the North Lot waitlist and book the FL3 room, do not ask for a live agent transfer. You may naturally ask once to be transferred only if help is needed to complete any actionable part. If you ask for transfer and are told transfer is not possible, accept that and continue with the available resolutions. Do not ask more than twice.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation, unless you have not yet started the second intent of booking the conference room.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If the agent asks whether you have any required room equipment, say you have no required equipment.
If the agent asks whether another floor besides FL2 is acceptable for the room booking, say yes, FL3 is acceptable only after FL2 is confirmed unavailable.
If the agent asks for the building for either request, say Headquarters.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: Your work laptop will not boot at all, and after troubleshooting you need the issue logged with a technician dispatched to your office. Since the laptop is also at end of life, you also want to request a Windows 14-inch replacement laptop and a new 27-inch monitor bundle for your setup.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few IT things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your dead laptop issue is formally logged, you receive an incident ticket number, you are told the SLA response target, and you receive a field dispatch confirmation for an on-site technician visit.
Your replacement laptop request is successfully submitted and you receive a hardware request ID along with return instructions and a return deadline for the old laptop.
Your 27-inch monitor bundle request is successfully submitted and you receive a hardware request ID for it.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number, and do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.

2. After verification, say you have three needs: your laptop will not boot, you need help with that first, and then you also need a replacement laptop and a monitor. Do not give the detailed specifications until each item is handled in turn.

3. First intent - hardware malfunction: when asked about the issue, state that your laptop will not boot at all. Follow the troubleshooting steps exactly as presented, one at a time, and after each step say the problem is still not resolved unless the agent explicitly asks whether it started working; in that case, say it still will not boot.
4. If the agent asks about business impact or urgency for the malfunction, say you are fully blocked because your main work laptop is unusable and you do not have a workaround.

5. If the agent asks for dispatch details, provide Headquarters and FL2. If asked for your preferred appointment, say 2026-04-15 in the afternoon. If 2026-04-15 afternoon is available, accept it. If that exact window is unavailable, accept the earliest offered time on 2026-04-15. If no slot is available on 2026-04-15, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date.

6. After the first intent is completed with a ticket number, SLA, and dispatch confirmation, move to the second intent and say you also need a replacement laptop because the current one is end-of-life.

7. Second intent - laptop replacement: provide details only when asked. State the justification is end_of_life. If asked for preferences, choose Windows and 14-inch. If asked for delivery location, provide Headquarters and FL2. If the agent reads back the request details, confirm them exactly as read if they match your requested OS, size, and location.

8. If the agent offers alternatives for laptop model, configuration, or size beyond the required OS and screen size, do not evaluate them; repeat that you want Windows and 14-inch, and otherwise accept the standard replacement that matches those two requirements.
9. After the laptop replacement is submitted, stay on the call until you receive the request ID and the return deadline and instructions for the old laptop.
10. Then move to the third intent and say you also need a monitor bundle for a new setup.

11. Third intent - monitor bundle: when asked, state the justification is new_setup. If asked for size, choose 27-inch. If asked for delivery location, provide Headquarters and FL2. If the agent reads back the monitor request, confirm it only if it is for a 27-inch monitor bundle delivered to Headquarters FL2.
12. If the agent offers multiple monitor options, do not compare them; repeat that you want the standard 27-inch monitor bundle and accept the matching standard option.

13. At the end, make sure all three intents have concrete outcomes: incident ticket number plus dispatch confirmation and SLA for the malfunction, a request ID plus return deadline for the replacement laptop, and a request ID for the monitor bundle. Then thank the agent and end the call.

Guiding behavior: never volunteer information before it is requested; answer only the specific question asked. If the agent reads back any IDs, dates, or delivery details, confirm only if they exactly match the values in information_required. If the agent asks an unexpected question not covered here, answer briefly using only the facts in information_required and do not invent anything.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received an incident ticket number for the dead laptop, the dispatch confirmation details, and the SLA response target; you have also received a hardware request ID for the replacement laptop plus the return deadline for the old device; and you have received a hardware request ID for the 27-inch monitor bundle. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If asked which operating system you want on the replacement laptop, choose Windows.
If asked what laptop size you want, choose 14-inch.
If asked what monitor size you want, choose 27-inch.

Reboot the workstation and test network access again.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to report that the HR portal is down because it loads but then times out, and you want the issue formally logged and tracked so you know engineering is aware of it.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, the HR portal seems to be down for me.

must_have_criteria: Your report of the HR portal outage is logged as a new incident, you are given the incident ticket number, and you are told the expected response timeline or SLA.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by briefly saying the HR portal is down for you, then wait for the agent to ask for verification details before giving your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number.
2. After verification, describe the issue only when asked: say the HR portal page loads but then times out and is completely inaccessible.
3. If the agent asks clarifying questions, answer narrowly and consistently: say the affected service is the HR portal, the main symptom is timeout, and you are unable to use it.
4. If the agent asks about impact, say you are blocked from using the HR portal and have no workaround.
5. If the agent says there is no existing outage and they will open a new incident, accept that and wait for the ticket details.
6. If the agent says engineering is aware through a known error but there is no workaround yet, acknowledge that and ask for the incident ticket number if they have not already provided it.

7. If the agent reads back any identifying details, the affected service, or the ticket number, confirm them if they match the information in this goal; otherwise correct only the specific incorrect item.
8. Do not volunteer extra technical details, guesses about root cause, or unrelated problems unless the agent directly asks.

9. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant questions not covered here, answer with the minimum information needed, staying consistent with the issue description that the HR portal loads and then times out.
10. Once you have the incident ticket number and the agent has explained that engineering is aware and a workaround will be posted once available, confirm understanding and wrap up.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received an incident ticket number for the HR portal outage, and the agent has told you the expected response timeline or SLA and that engineering is aware with no workaround currently available. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If asked whether other systems are affected, say you are only reporting the HR portal problem and do not add any other outage claims.
If asked whether there is a workaround available to you, say no.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have a few workplace requests to finish in one call: you need to replace your current laptop with a 13-inch Mac, request a standing desk converter for your desk, and book a conference room at Headquarters for your team standup this Friday morning.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few things I need help with today.

must_have_criteria: Your laptop replacement request is successfully submitted, you receive the request ID, and you are told the return instructions and the return deadline for your current laptop.
Your standing desk converter request is successfully submitted and you receive the request ID.
Your conference room is successfully booked for your standup and you receive the booking request ID or confirmation plus confirmation that the calendar invite was sent.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification only when asked. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number, and do not volunteer other details before the agent asks.

2. When asked what you need, say you have three requests: a replacement laptop, a standing desk converter, and a conference room booking. Then handle them one at a time in the order the agent chooses, but do not interleave details across intents unless asked.

3. For the laptop replacement intent, wait for the agent to ask for the reason and details. State that you need a replacement laptop and, if asked for justification, say it is end_of_life. If asked for operating system, say macos. If asked for size, say 13_inch. If asked for delivery location, say Headquarters, FL2. If the agent reads back details before submitting, confirm only if they exactly match the information_required values.

4. For the laptop replacement response handling, accept the successful eligible path only after the request is actually submitted and a request ID is given. Stay on the call until the return step is also completed and you are told the return deadline.

5. After the laptop replacement is fully handled, move to the standing desk converter. Wait for the agent to ask what equipment you want, then say standing_desk_converter. If asked for delivery location, say Headquarters, FL2. If asked about ergonomic assessment, say you believe one is already on file and let the agent verify it. Do not invent assessment dates or medical details.

6. For the standing desk converter response handling, accept the request only after the equipment request is actually submitted and a request ID is provided. If the agent mentions optional alternative ergonomic items, decline them and stay with the standing desk converter only.

7. After the equipment request is fully handled, move to the conference room booking. Wait for the agent to ask for the details, then provide: Headquarters, 2026-05-08, 09:00 to 09:30, 6 attendees. If asked for room equipment requirements, say you have no specific equipment requirements. If asked for a floor preference, say you do not have one.

8. For room selection, use this deterministic rule: if any available room at Headquarters is offered for 2026-05-08 from 09:00 to 09:30 with capacity of at least 6, choose the room with the smallest room code in alphabetical order among the options the agent presents in that turn. If the exact 09:00 to 09:30 slot is unavailable, accept the earliest offered start time on 2026-05-08 that still allows a 30-minute meeting in Headquarters with capacity of at least 6. If no room is available anywhere in Headquarters on 2026-05-08, accept the earliest offered 30-minute slot on the nearest future date in Headquarters with capacity of at least 6.

9. If the agent reads back the chosen room, date, and time before booking, confirm only if they exactly match the selected option under the rule above. Stay on the call until the booking is actually completed and the calendar invite has been sent or explicitly confirmed as sent, along with a booking request ID or confirmation.

10. At the end, make sure all three intents have concrete outcomes with identifiers: laptop replacement request ID plus return deadline, standing desk converter request ID, and conference room booking confirmation plus calendar invite confirmation. Once all are complete, thank the agent and end the call.

Guiding behavior: never volunteer all stored details at once; provide each value only when asked. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer only with facts from information_required or directly implied by the scenario. If the agent reads back any identifier, date, time, building, or floor, confirm it only if it matches exactly. Do not invent missing codes, asset tags, room codes, or assessment dates.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a request ID for your laptop replacement, been told the return instructions and return deadline for your old laptop, received a request ID for your standing desk converter, and received a booking confirmation or request ID for the conference room with confirmation that the calendar invite was sent. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If asked which operating system you want on the replacement laptop, choose macos.
If asked what laptop size you want, choose 13_inch.
If asked for the laptop replacement reason, choose end_of_life.
If asked for conference room equipment requirements, say there are no specific equipment requirements.
If asked for a conference room floor preference, say you do not have one.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have three access-related tasks to complete in one call: join the Database Read Access group, update your permissions for your HR-approved role change from Software Engineer to Engineering Manager, and get read-only access to Confluence.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few access requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your request to join the Database Read Access group is submitted and you receive the case or request ID for it, and if approval is required you are told it has been routed for approval.
Your permission change for your HR-approved move to Engineering Manager is submitted using the approved effective date, you receive the case ID, and the required 90-day access review is scheduled.
Your read-only Confluence access request is submitted successfully and you receive the request ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving a high-level overview only: say you have three access-related requests. Do not volunteer IDs, codes, role details, group details, or application details until the agent asks.

2. Complete authentication first. When asked, provide your employee ID, then the last four digits of your phone number, then the OTP code only after the agent asks for it. If the agent asks you to confirm your identity details by read-back, simply confirm they are correct.

3. Handle the first intent as the group membership change. When the agent asks what group you need, say you want to be added to the Database Read Access group. If asked for the group code, provide GRP-DBREAD. If asked whether this is an add or remove request, say add. If the agent explains approval is required, accept that and wait for the submission to be completed and routed. Do not ask for faster approval or exceptions.

4. After the first intent is completed or explicitly submitted, move to the second intent. Say you also need your permissions updated for your HR-approved role change. When asked for your old and new roles, say your current role is SWE and your new role is MGENG, Engineering Manager. When asked for the effective date, provide 2026-08-31. If the agent presents permission templates for the new role, choose PTPL-MGENG-01. If the agent reads the template back before submission, confirm it is correct. Stay on the call until the permission change is submitted and the access review is scheduled.

5. After the second intent is completed, move to the third intent. Say you also need Confluence access. When asked for the application name, say Confluence. When asked for the access level, say read_only. If the agent says it is auto-approved, accept that and wait for the request to be fully submitted. If the agent reads back the application name or access level, confirm it is correct.
6. Once all three intents have concrete completion outcomes and reference numbers, confirm that everything sounds right and end the call.

At every confirmation step before submission, answer yes if the agent's summary exactly matches the information in this goal. If any single item is read back incorrectly, correct only that item and do not add extra explanation.

If the agent asks unexpected but relevant follow-up questions that are directly necessary to complete one of the three intents, answer only with the exact value from information_required when available. If the question asks for a preference not defined here, say you do not have any additional preference and want the standard option for that request.

Do not interleave unresolved intents yourself. Let the agent finish one request before moving to the next. If the agent asks what else you need after finishing one intent, then introduce the next intent.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need help with a VPN connection that keeps disconnecting and does not get fixed through troubleshooting, so you want an incident ticket, the diagnostic attached, and the SLA response time confirmed. You also want a desk at Headquarters; if floor 2 is unavailable, you will take floor 3 instead. You also want a parking space in North Lot.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your VPN issue is ticketed, your diagnostic reference code is attached to the incident, and you receive the incident ticket number plus the SLA response time.
You receive a confirmed desk assignment at Headquarters on floor 3 with the assigned desk code.
You receive a confirmed parking assignment in North Lot with the assigned parking space ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want a desk on floor 2 of Headquarters, but if you are told no desks are available there, ask one follow-up question about alternatives and then accept floor 3 instead.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number when asked. If asked what you need help with, say you have three things: a VPN issue, a desk request, and parking, but do not give detailed specifics until the agent asks.

2. First intent — VPN issue: Describe the problem as your VPN keeps dropping repeatedly. If the agent asks clarifying questions, say it connects and then disconnects intermittently rather than failing a login, and do not claim other employees are affected unless directly asked; if asked whether others are seeing it too, say you do not know. Follow the troubleshooting steps in order and, after each step, say the issue is still happening. When asked for the diagnostic code after troubleshooting fails, provide the diagnostic reference code exactly as listed in information_required. If asked about business impact or urgency, say you are blocked from working whenever the VPN disconnects. When the agent summarizes the ticket details, confirm them.

3. Second intent — desk request: Ask for a desk at Headquarters on floor 2. If the agent says floor 2 has no availability, ask exactly one follow-up question: "Is there anything else available in Headquarters?" If an alternative in Headquarters on floor 3 is available, accept floor 3. If multiple floor 3 desks are offered, choose the desk with the lexicographically smallest desk code as read by the agent. If only one floor 3 desk is offered, accept it. Do not ask for a waitlist once a floor 3 option is available. Confirm the final selected desk when the agent reads it back.

4. Third intent — parking request: Ask for parking in North Lot. If the agent offers multiple available spaces in North Lot, choose the parking space with the lexicographically smallest space ID as read by the agent. If only one North Lot space is offered, accept it. Confirm the selected parking space when the agent reads it back.

5. Wrap up only after all three intents have been handled. Before ending, make sure you have the VPN incident ticket number and SLA response time, the desk assignment confirmation with desk code, and the parking assignment confirmation with parking space ID. If the agent asks if there is anything else, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received an incident ticket number for the VPN issue, your diagnostic reference has been attached, and the SLA response time has been communicated; you have been told floor 2 at Headquarters was unavailable and you have received a confirmed floor 3 desk assignment with a desk code; and you have received a confirmed North Lot parking assignment with a parking space ID. End the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need your locked Active Directory account unlocked so you can log in again, and once that is fixed, you also want a 30-day Adobe Creative Cloud trial and a footrest delivered to your desk location.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your Active Directory account is unlocked and you are told it has been successfully resolved.
Your 30-day Adobe Creative Cloud temporary license request is submitted and you receive a request ID or confirmation including the temporary duration.
Your footrest request is submitted for delivery to your location and you receive a request ID or confirmation.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by giving only a high-level overview that you have multiple requests, and complete identity verification when asked using your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number. Do not volunteer detailed identifiers until the agent asks for them.

2. For the first intent, say you are locked out of your Active Directory account only after the agent asks what the first issue is. If asked clarifying questions, confirm that the problem is specifically that the account is locked, not that you forgot the password and not that another service is down.

3. If the agent reads back the system as Active Directory or says they can attempt an unlock, confirm that is correct. If asked to confirm before the unlock action, say yes. After the agent says it succeeded, acknowledge that the login issue is fixed and move to the next request.

4. For the second intent, only after the first intent is completed, say you also want a 30-day trial of Adobe Creative Cloud. If asked for the product name, say exactly "Adobe Creative Cloud." If asked whether you want permanent or temporary, say temporary. If asked for duration, say 30 days.

5. If the agent reads back the product name or duration, confirm exactly what they said if it matches Adobe Creative Cloud for 30 days. If the agent offers only allowed duration options and somehow asks you to choose again, choose 30 days. If the agent says the request can be submitted, explicitly confirm that they should proceed. Wait for a request ID or equivalent confirmation before moving on.

6. For the third intent, only after the license request is completed, say you also need a footrest for your desk. If asked what kind of equipment, say exactly "footrest." If asked for delivery location, provide your building and floor only when asked.

7. If the agent asks whether an ergonomic assessment exists or is needed, state that you are requesting a footrest. Do not ask for any other equipment. If the agent is ready to submit the footrest request, confirm they should proceed and wait for the request ID or equivalent confirmation.

8. At the end, if the agent summarizes all completed actions, confirm the summary if it matches what happened. If any request ID, case ID, or confirmation number is read back, acknowledge it without altering it. After all three intents are completed, thank the agent and end the call.

Guiding behavioral directions: Do not volunteer details before the agent asks for them. When asked for a value, provide exactly the value from information_required and nothing extra. If the agent reads back a name, ID, duration, building, or floor and it matches, say yes. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer briefly using only the defined scenario facts. If the agent asks you to choose among equivalent valid phrasings for the same request, always restate the exact requested item: Active Directory unlock, Adobe Creative Cloud temporary 30-day license, and footrest.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have a few IT issues to handle in one call: the CRM platform appears to be down, your Active Directory account is locked and you want it unlocked, and you also want read-only access to Confluence.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few IT things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your report that the CRM platform is down is logged, and you receive an incident ticket number plus the stated SLA response timeframe.
Your Confluence read-only access request is submitted successfully, and you receive a request ID or confirmation that the access request has been completed.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your Active Directory account unlocked during the call, but if you are told that it cannot be unlocked because of a security hold, ask one follow-up question and then accept the outcome as long as a login incident ticket is created and you are given the SLA response timeframe.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Begin by completing identity verification first. When asked, provide your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number. Do not volunteer other details until the agent asks.

2. After verification, give a high-level overview that you have three items: the CRM platform seems down, your Active Directory account is locked, and you also need Confluence read-only access. Do not describe all details at once unless the agent asks.

3. Handle the first intent completely before moving on. For the CRM outage, describe only that the CRM platform is down for you and wait for the agent's follow-up questions. If asked whether others are affected, say you do not know. If asked whether this blocks your work, say yes, because you cannot use CRM right now.

4. For the CRM outage intent, accept the outage handling outcome only when the agent gives you a concrete incident ticket number and the expected SLA or response timeframe. If the agent mentions a known error or workaround, listen and acknowledge it; do not invent extra symptoms.

5. After the CRM issue is handled, move to the second intent. State that your Active Directory account is locked and you want it unlocked. If asked for the target system, say Active Directory. Do not claim a password-expired issue; this is specifically a lockout.

6. For the Active Directory lockout intent, if the agent says they cannot unlock it because of a security hold, ask exactly one follow-up question: 'Is there anything else that can be done today?' After that single follow-up, accept the denial as long as a login incident ticket is created and you are told the SLA response timeframe.

7. Because the scenario calls for live-agent escalation, after the clear denial of the unlock request and after receiving the ticket/SLA information, ask naturally to be transferred to a live agent. If the agent says transfer is not possible, accept that and continue with the remaining request without insisting more than once.

8. After the login issue is addressed, move to the third intent. Request access to Confluence and wait for the agent to ask for the access level. When asked, specify read_only exactly. Do not ask for any higher level.

9. For the Confluence request, accept the result only when the request is actually submitted or completed and you are given a concrete request ID or confirmation identifier. If the agent says approval is not needed, simply acknowledge that. Do not add any extra applications.

10. At the end of the call, briefly confirm the outcome of all three items: the CRM outage ticket, the Active Directory login ticket and any transfer outcome, and the Confluence access request confirmation. Then end the call.

Guiding behavioral directions: never volunteer identifiers, access levels, or system names before the agent asks for them, except for the initial high-level overview. If the agent reads back any name, ID, or request details, confirm them exactly if they match the information_required fields. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarifying question, answer briefly using only the facts defined here. If the agent asks for information not defined here, say you do not have additional details.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a concrete incident ticket number and SLA timeframe for the CRM outage, you have been told your Active Directory account cannot be unlocked because of a security hold and have received a concrete login incident ticket number plus SLA timeframe for that issue, you have requested a live-agent transfer after that denial and either been transferred or clearly told transfer is not possible, and you have received a concrete request ID or confirmation for your Confluence read-only access request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: After the Active Directory unlock request is clearly denied because of the security hold, request transfer to a live agent naturally. Accept the transfer if offered. If told transfer is not possible, accept that outcome and do not insist more than once.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If asked which application you want access to, say Confluence.
If asked what access level you need for that application, say read_only.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to complete a full refresh in one call: replace your current laptop, request a new 32-inch monitor bundle, and order an ergonomic chair for delivery to your office location.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few equipment requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your replacement laptop request is successfully submitted and you receive a hardware request ID, and you are told the return deadline for your current laptop.
Your 32-inch monitor bundle request is successfully submitted and you receive a hardware request ID.
Your ergonomic chair request is successfully submitted and you receive an equipment request ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing identity verification with your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number. After verification, say you have three requests: a replacement laptop, a monitor, and an ergonomic chair. Do not volunteer detailed specifications until the agent asks.

2. First intent — replacement laptop: when asked, say this is a refresh of your current laptop and the justification is end_of_life. If asked for operating system, choose windows. If asked for screen size, choose 16_inch. If asked for delivery location, give Headquarters and FL3. If the agent reads back the request details, confirm only if they match exactly. Stay on this intent until you receive the laptop request ID and the return deadline for the old laptop.

3. Second intent — monitor bundle: after the laptop request is completed, say you also need a monitor. When asked, say it is a replacement request and the size is 32_inch. If asked for delivery location, give Headquarters and FL3. If the agent reads back the request details, confirm only if they match exactly. Stay on this intent until you receive the monitor request ID.

4. Third intent — ergonomic chair: after the monitor request is completed, say you also want an ergonomic chair. If asked for delivery location, give Headquarters and FL3. If the agent asks about the required ergonomic pre-check, answer that you have a completed ergonomic assessment on file. If the agent reads back the request details, confirm only if they match exactly. Stay on this intent until you receive the equipment request ID.

5. For all three intents, do not volunteer extra information beyond what the agent asks for. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer briefly using only the values in information_required. If the agent summarizes all completed requests at the end, confirm the summary only if each request outcome and ID type is clearly stated, then end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a request ID for your laptop replacement, been told the return deadline for your current laptop, received a request ID for your 32-inch monitor bundle, and received a request ID for your ergonomic chair request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: Your laptop screen is cracked and you need IT to log the hardware issue, arrange a technician visit, and also submit a replacement laptop request because the device is damaged. While that is being handled, you also want to book a conference room for a team huddle tomorrow so your team can coordinate work.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few things I need help with about my laptop and a room booking.

must_have_criteria: Your cracked laptop screen issue is formally logged and you receive an incident ticket number, a dispatch confirmation for a technician visit, and the expected SLA response timing.
Your replacement laptop request is submitted successfully and you receive a hardware request ID along with the return instructions and return deadline for your current laptop.
Your conference room is booked successfully and you receive the room booking confirmation details and calendar invite confirmation.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Give your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number when asked. Do not volunteer detailed request information until the agent asks.

2. After verification, say you have three needs: a damaged laptop screen issue, a replacement laptop request, and a conference room booking. Then let the agent handle them one at a time in that order.

3. For the first intent, describe only that your laptop screen is cracked and the laptop is company-issued. If the agent asks clarifying questions, answer directly and keep the problem limited to the damaged screen. Follow all troubleshooting steps in order and, after each step, state clearly that the issue is still not resolved.

4. When describing the problem, proactively mention that the damaged screen is blocking normal work on the laptop, without waiting to be asked. If the agent asks about business impact, restate this directly. If the agent asks for dispatch location details, provide the building and floor from your information_required values only when asked.

5. For dispatch scheduling, first ask for the preferred date 2026-05-20 and the morning window. If that exact option is unavailable, accept the earliest offered slot on 2026-05-20. If no slot is available on 2026-05-20, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date. If multiple options are offered on the same qualifying date, choose the earliest time or earliest window offered.

6. After the first intent is completed with a ticket number, dispatch confirmation, and SLA timing, move to the second intent and say you also need a replacement laptop because of the physical damage. Provide OS, size, and delivery location only when the agent asks.

7. For the replacement laptop request, if asked for configuration, choose macos and 14_inch exactly. If asked for justification, state physical damage. If asked for delivery location, provide Headquarters and FL2. If the agent reads back the request details, confirm them exactly if they match your information_required values.

8. Accept the replacement request once it is actually submitted and you are given a request ID plus the return deadline/instructions for the current laptop. Do not end the call on a promise to submit it later.

9. After the replacement request is completed, move to the third intent and ask to book a conference room for your team coordination meeting. Provide the meeting date, start time, end time, attendee count, and preferred building only when asked. Do not add any equipment requirements unless the agent specifically asks; if asked, say you have no required equipment.

10. For the room booking, first request 2026-05-19 from 10:00 to 12:00 in Headquarters for 4 attendees. If no room is available for that exact building and time, accept the earliest room the agent offers in Headquarters on 2026-05-19 that fully covers the 10:00 to 12:00 window. If none are available in Headquarters for that window, accept the earliest offered room on the nearest future date at the same 10:00 to 12:00 time window. If multiple rooms satisfy the same date and time option, choose the room code that comes first alphabetically as read by the agent.

11. When the agent reads back any IDs, dates, room codes, or appointment details, listen and confirm only if they match what was submitted or booked. If a read-back differs from your requested values or a confirmed selection, correct only the mismatched field.

12. End the call only after all three intents have reached concrete outcomes: the hardware incident has a ticket number and dispatch confirmation with SLA, the laptop replacement has a request ID and return instructions, and the room booking has a booking confirmation and calendar invite record.

End the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have three software requests to complete in one call: read-only access to Confluence, standard access to Datadog, and a 90-day IntelliJ trial. You want each request actually submitted during the call, including any approval routing that is required.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few software requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your read-only Confluence access request is submitted successfully and you receive a request ID or clear confirmation number for it.
Your standard Datadog access request is submitted successfully, any required manager approval is routed during the call, and you receive a request ID or clear confirmation number for it.
Your 90-day IntelliJ trial request is submitted successfully and you receive a request ID or clear confirmation number for it, along with the trial end date.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard identity verification when asked, using your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number. Do not volunteer those details before the agent asks. After verification, say you have three software requests.

2. Handle the first intent completely before moving to the next. For the first intent, if the agent asks what application you need, say Confluence. If the agent asks what access level you need, say read_only. If the agent reads the request back, confirm it exactly if it matches.

3. After the Confluence request is fully handled, state the second intent only when the agent asks for the next request or asks if there is anything else. For the second intent, if the agent asks what application you need, say Datadog. If the agent asks what access level you need, say standard. If the agent says approval is required, accept that and wait for the request to be submitted and routed. If the agent reads the details back, confirm them exactly if they match.

4. After the Datadog request is fully handled, state the third intent only when the agent asks for the next request or asks if there is anything else. For the third intent, if the agent asks what license you need, say IntelliJ. If the agent asks whether this is permanent or temporary, say temporary. If the agent asks for the duration, say 90 days. If the agent reads the details back, confirm them exactly if they match.

5. Before ending the call, make sure each of the three requests has been completed with a concrete outcome. Stay on the call until you have a request ID or other explicit confirmation for Confluence, a request ID or other explicit confirmation for Datadog plus confirmation that approval routing was done if required, and a request ID or other explicit confirmation for the IntelliJ trial plus the expiration date. Then thank the agent and end the call.

Guiding behavior: Do not volunteer detailed product names, access levels, or duration until the agent asks for each one. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant clarification question, answer only with the exact value from information_required. If the agent misreads any name, access level, or duration, correct only the incorrect field and nothing else. If the agent offers unnecessary choices that are not defined in your goal, ask them to use the standard/default option for the requested product if one exists; otherwise repeat only the exact request you are making.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a request ID or explicit confirmation for your read-only Confluence request, a request ID or explicit confirmation for your standard Datadog request and confirmation that any required approval routing was completed, and a request ID or explicit confirmation for your 90-day IntelliJ trial along with the trial expiration date of 2026-12-06. End the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: Your external monitor suddenly shows no signal even though it was working yesterday, and you want the help desk to walk you through fixing it so you can get it working again during the call.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, my external monitor says no signal and I need help getting it working again.

must_have_criteria: Your monitor issue is fixed during the call through troubleshooting, and the agent clearly confirms the issue was resolved without needing a ticket.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by briefly stating that your external monitor shows no signal. Do not volunteer identifying details until the agent asks for them.
2. When asked to verify your identity, provide your employee ID and phone last four exactly as listed in information_required.
3. When asked for symptoms, say only that the external monitor shows no signal and that it was working yesterday. Do not add extra troubleshooting steps unless the agent asks.

4. Follow the hardware troubleshooting guide one step at a time in the order the agent gives it. After each step, if the agent asks whether the issue is resolved, say it is not resolved after steps 1 and 2.
5. When the agent gives the cable-reseating step, say you reseated the DisplayPort cable on both ends. If the agent then asks whether the monitor works now, say yes, it is working now.
6. If the agent asks what fixed it, say reseating the DisplayPort cable on both ends fixed it.
7. Stay on the call until the agent explicitly confirms the issue has been marked resolved or otherwise clearly closed without a ticket. Then confirm there is nothing else needed and wrap up.
Behavior rule: Do not volunteer step numbers, cable type, or prior actions unless the agent asks. Only confirm the specific successful outcome when asked after the third troubleshooting step.

Behavior rule: If the agent reads back your name, employee ID, or issue summary, confirm only if it matches the information_required and scenario details exactly; otherwise correct only the incorrect item.

Behavior rule: If the agent asks unexpected but relevant troubleshooting questions, answer minimally and consistently with the scenario: the issue is only that the external monitor shows no signal and it worked yesterday.
Behavior rule: Do not ask for a replacement monitor, a technician visit, or a ticket if the troubleshooting succeeds.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: The external monitor is working again after reseating the DisplayPort cable on both ends, and the agent has clearly confirmed the issue was marked resolved with no ticket needed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, information, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit IT security in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated IT request.
If the agent asks which monitor connection is in use, say DisplayPort.
If the agent asks whether the monitor worked before today, say yes, it was working yesterday.
If the agent asks whether the computer itself is on, say yes unless the scenario has already advanced to the successful cable-reseating step.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are setting up a new office arrangement at Headquarters. You want to try for a desk on floor 2 first, accept a desk on floor 3 if floor 2 is unavailable, request an ergonomic chair because you already have an assessment on file, and book a conference room for next Wednesday from 11:00 to 12:00 for 8 people.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few office setup things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your desk request is completed with a confirmed desk assignment on floor 3 at Headquarters, and you receive a desk assignment confirmation or request ID.
Your ergonomic chair request is submitted for delivery to Headquarters floor 3, and you receive a request ID.

Your conference room booking for 2026-06-03 from 11:00 to 12:00 at Headquarters for 8 attendees is confirmed, and you receive a booking confirmation or request ID plus confirmation that the calendar invite was sent.
nice_to_have_criteria: You would like a desk on floor 2 at Headquarters, but if you are told no desks are available there, ask one follow-up question about alternatives and then accept a desk on floor 3 instead.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Give your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number when asked. Do not volunteer other details before authentication is complete.

2. After verification, say you need help with three things: a desk assignment, an ergonomic chair, and a conference room booking. Do not give the detailed preferences until the agent asks about each request.

3. First intent - desk assignment: if asked for your preference, say you want Headquarters floor FL2. If the agent says floor 2 has no availability, ask one follow-up question: whether there is anything available on another floor in Headquarters. If an alternative is offered, accept FL3 at Headquarters. If multiple FL3 desks are offered, choose the first desk code the agent presents from the FL3 options. If the agent offers a desk outside Headquarters before offering FL3 at Headquarters, decline that and restate that your fallback is FL3 at Headquarters.

4. Second intent - ergonomic chair: when asked, request an ergonomic chair and provide the delivery location as Headquarters, floor FL3. If asked whether you have an ergonomic assessment on file, say yes. If asked any optional preference not specified in this goal, say you have no additional preference and want the standard ergonomic chair option.

5. Third intent - conference room booking: when asked for details, provide Headquarters, date 2026-06-03, start time 11:00, end time 12:00, and 8 attendees. Do not request any room equipment unless the agent specifically asks; if asked, say you have no required equipment. If multiple available rooms are offered that meet the request, choose the first room the agent presents. If no room is available exactly at that time, accept the earliest offered start time on 2026-06-03 that begins at or after 11:00 and preserves a 1-hour meeting duration. If no qualifying slot is available on 2026-06-03, accept the earliest offered 1-hour slot on the nearest future date in Headquarters.
6. When the agent reads back any desk code, room code, confirmation number, or request ID, listen and confirm it if it matches what they said. Do not add new requirements.

7. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant questions not covered above, answer only with the exact information in information_required. If the question asks for a preference not defined there, say you have no additional preference.
8. Finish only after all three intents have been handled: floor-2 denial acknowledged with floor-3 desk completed, ergonomic chair submitted, and conference room booked with confirmation details.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a confirmed desk assignment on Headquarters floor 3 with a confirmation or request ID, your ergonomic chair request for Headquarters FL3 has been submitted with a request ID, and your conference room for 2026-06-03 from 11:00 to 12:00 for 8 attendees at Headquarters has been booked with a booking confirmation or request ID and calendar invite confirmation. The floor 2 desk request has been clearly denied or found unavailable and you have accepted the floor 3 fallback. End the call.

decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have a few IT requests to complete in one call: you want a 24-inch monitor bundle delivered to your East Campus floor, standard access to Confluence, and a 30-day Figma trial license.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a few IT requests I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your 24-inch monitor bundle request is successfully submitted and you receive a hardware request ID.
Your standard Confluence access request is successfully submitted and you receive an access request ID or confirmation number showing the request was created.
Your 30-day Figma trial request is successfully submitted and you receive a license request ID, along with confirmation that it is for 30 days.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by asking for help with a few IT requests. Do not volunteer detailed specifics until the agent asks. When asked to verify your identity, provide your employee ID and then your phone last four digits exactly as listed in information_required.

2. Handle the first intent completely before moving on. For the monitor request, state that you want a monitor bundle when asked what hardware you need. If asked for the size, say 24-inch. If asked why you need it, say it is for a new setup. If asked for the delivery location, provide the delivery building and then the delivery floor exactly as listed in information_required. If the agent summarizes the request, confirm it only if it matches information_required.

3. After the monitor request is fully handled, raise the second intent. Say you also need access to Confluence when the agent asks what else you need. If asked for the access level, say standard. If the agent reads back the application name and access level, confirm it only if it matches information_required.

4. After the Confluence request is fully handled, raise the third intent. Say you also need a Figma trial when the agent asks what else you need. If asked how long, say 30 days. If the agent offers duration choices or asks you to confirm the duration, choose 30 days exactly. If the agent reads back the license request, confirm it only if it matches information_required.

5. Before ending the call, make sure each of the three intents has a concrete completed outcome with a request ID or confirmation number. If one intent has been skipped, remind the agent of that specific remaining request.

Do not volunteer catalog IDs, approval logic, cost center details, or internal process suggestions. If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions, answer only with the exact value from information_required if one exists; otherwise say you only need what you already requested.

For any read-back of your personal details or request details, answer briefly and confirm only the parts that match information_required. If something is incorrect, correct only the incorrect field and do not add or invent new information.

5. Second intent — desk assignment: ask for a desk at Headquarters on floor 2 only after the Wi-Fi issue is finished. Provide the building and floor only when asked.

6. If the agent says there is nothing available on floor 2, ask exactly one follow-up question asking whether there is any desk available at all on floor 2. If the answer is still no, immediately ask for Headquarters floor 3 instead.

7. If the agent offers multiple desk options on floor 3, choose the desk with the lexicographically smallest desk code exactly as read by the agent. If only one floor 3 desk is offered, accept it. Do not choose any desk on a floor other than floor 3.
8. Confirm the selected floor 3 desk assignment only after the agent reads back the building, floor, and desk code or assignment details.
9. Third intent — footrest: after the desk request is completed, ask for a footrest. Provide the delivery building and floor only when asked.

10. If the agent asks whether you need any other ergonomic item instead of or in addition to the footrest, say no, only a footrest. If the agent asks about ergonomic assessment, say you are requesting a footrest and proceed; do not volunteer anything else.
11. Accept the footrest request once the agent gives you a request ID or equivalent confirmation and reads back the delivery location as Headquarters floor 3.
For any read-back of names, IDs, floors, building names, or request details, confirm only if they match exactly what is in information_required; otherwise correct the specific field and nothing more.

If the agent asks unexpected but relevant clarifying questions not covered here, answer briefly using only the information in information_required. Do not invent symptoms, dates, preferences, or alternative locations.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been told your Wi-Fi issue is resolved, you have received a confirmed desk assignment at Headquarters on floor 3 after floor 2 was unavailable, and you have received a request ID or confirmation for a footrest delivery to Headquarters floor 3. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have a few IT tasks to finish in one call: first get your locked Active Directory account unlocked, then request a replacement laptop because your current one has reached end of life, and finally request read-only access to Confluence.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I've got a couple of IT things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: Your Active Directory account is unlocked and the agent confirms it has been resolved.
Your laptop replacement request is submitted successfully, you receive the hardware request ID, and you are told the return deadline and return instructions for your current laptop.
Your read-only Confluence access request is submitted successfully and you receive the request ID.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Give your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number when asked. Do not volunteer additional details before the agent asks.

2. When asked what you need help with, say you first need your Active Directory account unlocked, and mention only at a high level that you also have a laptop replacement request and an access request after that.

3. For the first intent, confirm that the problem is a locked account, not a password-expiration issue. If asked which system is locked, say Active Directory. If the agent asks troubleshooting questions, answer briefly and consistently that the account is locked and you need it unlocked.

4. After the unlock is completed, if the agent confirms it worked or that the issue is resolved, acknowledge that and move to the second intent. Do not revisit the login issue unless the agent asks a direct follow-up question.

5. For the second intent, say you need a replacement laptop because your current one is end of life. Provide details only when asked: justification is end_of_life, operating system is windows, screen size is 14_inch, delivery building is Headquarters, and delivery floor is FL2.

6. If the agent asks you to confirm the hardware request details before submission, confirm it only if it matches information_required. If the agent offers choices that include operating system or size, choose windows and 14_inch exactly; do not choose any alternative.

7. After the laptop request is submitted, stay on the call until you receive the hardware request ID and the return details for your old laptop, including the return deadline. If the agent only says they will process it later and gives no ID, ask once for the submitted request ID and return deadline.

8. After the laptop replacement is complete, move to the third intent and say you also need Confluence access. Provide details only when asked: application name is Confluence and access level is read_only.

9. If the agent asks what level of access you need, answer read_only exactly. If the agent reads back the application name or access level, confirm only if it matches Confluence and read_only exactly.

10. Stay on the call until the Confluence access request has actually been submitted and you receive the request ID. If the agent says it is auto-approved, accept that, but still wait for the concrete request confirmation.
11. If the agent reads back any identifier, date, or request details, listen and confirm if they match what you provided. If they do not match, correct only the specific wrong field and nothing else.

12. If the agent asks an unexpected but relevant question, answer briefly using only the information in information_required. If the question is irrelevant to these three intents or asks for information not defined here, say you only need help with the locked account, replacement laptop, and Confluence read-only access.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been told your Active Directory account has been unlocked and resolved, you have received a hardware request ID for the replacement laptop plus the return deadline and return instructions for your current device, and you have received a request ID for the read-only Confluence access request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

If asked what laptop size you want, choose 14_inch.
If asked for the access level on the application, choose read_only.
If asked which application you want access to, say Confluence.
If asked whether the login issue is a lockout or an expired password, choose locked account.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You have a few workspace setup tasks to complete in one call: you want a desk at Headquarters, preferably on floor 2 but floor 3 if floor 2 is unavailable, a parking space in the North Lot, an ergonomic chair delivered to Headquarters floor 3, and a conference room at Headquarters for a team intro meeting on 2026-03-13 from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM for 6 people.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I have a few workspace setup things I need help with.

must_have_criteria: You receive a confirmed desk assignment at Headquarters on floor 3 with a desk code or other concrete assignment confirmation.
You receive a confirmed parking assignment in the North Lot with a parking space ID or other concrete assignment confirmation.
You receive a confirmed ergonomic chair request for delivery to Headquarters floor 3 with a request ID.
You receive a confirmed conference room booking at Headquarters for 2026-03-13 from 14:00 to 15:00 for 6 attendees, plus a booking request ID and calendar event confirmation.
nice_to_have_criteria: You would like a desk on floor 2 at Headquarters, but if you are told floor 2 is not available, ask one follow-up question about alternatives and then accept floor 3 instead.
negotiation_behavior: 1. Start by completing standard verification only. Give your employee ID and the last four digits of your phone number only when asked. After verification, say you have several workspace setup requests, but do not list all details until the agent asks.

2. First intent: desk assignment. If asked what you want, say you want a desk at Headquarters and your preferred floor is second floor. If the agent says the second floor (FL2) is unavailable, ask one follow-up question: whether there are any alternatives. If the agent offers a desk on the third floor (FL3), choose the first desk code the agent reads out. If the agent reads back the selected desk, confirm it exactly.

3. Second intent: parking assignment. When the agent moves on or asks what else you need, say you need parking in the North Lot. If multiple North Lot spaces are offered, choose the first parking space ID the agent reads out. If no North Lot space is offered even though the scenario says one is available, ask the agent to re-check North Lot once; if still unavailable, follow the failure condition. If the agent reads back the chosen space, confirm it exactly.

4. Third intent: ergonomic chair. When asked for the item, request an ergonomic chair. If asked about assessment status, say you should already have an ergonomic assessment on file. If asked for delivery location, provide Headquarters, FL3. If the agent reads back the equipment type and delivery location, confirm them exactly.

5. Fourth intent: conference room booking. When asked for meeting details, provide Headquarters as the building, 2026-03-13 as the date, 14:00 as the start time, 15:00 as the end time, and 6 attendees. Do not volunteer any room equipment requirements unless asked; if asked, say you have no specific equipment requirements. If multiple available rooms are offered for that exact date and time, choose the first room code the agent reads out. If no rooms are available for 2026-03-13 14:00-15:00, ask the agent to check the same building on the same date for the earliest available later start time that still ends by 17:00; if multiple such options are offered, choose the earliest start time, and if there is still a tie, choose the first room code read out. If nothing is available on 2026-03-13, accept the earliest offered slot on the nearest future date after 2026-03-13, and if multiple options share that earliest date and time, choose the first room code read out.

6. Throughout the call, do not volunteer extra details before the agent asks. Answer direct questions briefly and exactly. When the agent reads back names, IDs, dates, floors, room codes, desk codes, or parking space IDs, confirm them if correct.

7. End only after every raised intent has either been completed with a concrete confirmation or explicitly denied and acknowledged. If the agent asks whether you need anything else after all four intents are handled, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a concrete outcome for every intent: floor 2 desk unavailability has been explicitly acknowledged, you have a confirmed desk assignment on floor 3 with an assignment identifier, a confirmed North Lot parking assignment with a space ID, a confirmed ergonomic chair request with a request ID, and a confirmed conference room booking for the meeting with both a booking request ID and calendar event confirmation. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your requests for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If told to visit IT security in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
